skill.name: self-improvement
skill.description: Captures learnings, errors, and corrections to enable continuous improvement. Use when: (1) A command or operation fails unexpectedly, (2) User corrects Claude ('No, that's wrong...', 'Actually...'), (3) User requests a capability that doesn't exist, (4) An external API or tool fails, (5) Claude realizes its knowledge is outdated or incorrect, (6) A better approach is discovered for a recurring task. Also review learnings before major tasks.
skill.summary: Captures learnings, errors, and corrections to enable continuous improvement for AI agents.
skill.author: peterskoett

security_audit.summary: All 440 static findings are false positives. The skill is a legitimate self-improvement system that logs learnings to local markdown files. The hook code is clean with proper safety checks. The documentation contains markdown code examples with backticks that the scanner misidentified as Ruby command execution. Security instructions advising NOT to log secrets were flagged as credential access, but this is a protective measure. No malicious code, data exfiltration, or command injection vulnerabilities exist.

medium_findings.title: Documentation Patterns Misidentified as Code Execution
medium_findings.description: Static scanner flagged 363 instances of backticks in markdown files as 'Ruby/shell backtick execution'. These are markdown inline code formatting used in documentation examples, not actual command execution. Verified by reading SKILL.md lines 13-636 which contain only documentation with example commands in code blocks.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Read actual markdown files and confirmed all backticks are in documentation contexts, not executable code. The skill files (.js, .ts, .sh) contain no backtick command execution.

content.user_title: Capture learnings and errors for continuous improvement
content.value_statement: AI agents forget mistakes between sessions. This skill captures errors, corrections, and insights to markdown files so agents improve over time and avoid repeating the same mistakes.
actual_capabilities: Log command failures to .learnings/ERRORS.md with context and suggested fixes
Record user corrections and knowledge gaps to .learnings/LEARNINGS.md
Promote broadly applicable learnings to project memory files
Detect recurring patterns and suggest systemic improvements
Generate new skills from learning entries via helper scripts
limitations: Only logs to local markdown files; requires manual review to be effective
Does not automatically fix errors; captures information for future reference
Requires the agent or user to review learnings before tasks for maximum benefit

use_cases.title: Error Documentation
use_cases.description: When a command fails or API call errors out, log the error with context so future sessions know how to handle it.
use_cases.target_user: Developers working on complex projects with many dependencies

use_cases.title: User Feedback Capture
use_cases.description: Record when the user corrects the AI or provides guidance, building a knowledge base of project-specific preferences.
use_cases.target_user: Teams collaborating with AI coding assistants

use_cases.title: Pattern Recognition
use_cases.description: Identify recurring issues across sessions and promote solutions to project-wide documentation.
use_cases.target_user: AI agents working long-term on evolving codebases

prompt_templates.title: Basic Error Logging
prompt_templates.prompt: This command failed: `npm install` with error: "EACCES: permission denied". Log this error to .learnings/ERRORS.md.

prompt_templates.title: User Correction Capture
prompt_templates.prompt: The user said "No, we use yarn not npm for this project". Log this as a correction in .learnings/LEARNINGS.md with category 'best_practice'.

prompt_templates.title: Feature Request Logging
prompt_templates.prompt: User asked to add dark mode but Claude cannot do that. Log this capability gap to .learnings/FEATURE_REQUESTS.md.

prompt_templates.title: Learning Promotion
prompt_templates.prompt: Review .learnings/LEARNINGS.md and promote any broadly applicable entries to CLAUDE.md for project-wide reference.

output_examples.input: Command `docker build` failed with platform error
output_examples.output:
## [ERR-20260417-001] docker_build_platform

**Logged**: 2026-04-17T14:00:00Z
**Priority**: medium
**Status**: pending
**Area**: infra

### Summary
Docker build fails on Apple Silicon due to platform mismatch

### Error
```
ERROR: invalid reference format: repository name must be lowercase
```

### Context
Build command used ARM architecture on M2 MacBook Pro

### Suggested Fix
Add `--platform linux/amd64` flag or use buildx for cross-platform builds

output_examples.input: User corrects approach: 'Use pytest not unittest'
output_examples.output:
## [LRN-20260417-001] correction

**Logged**: 2026-04-17T14:00:00Z
**Priority**: low
**Status**: pending
**Area**: tests

### Summary
Project uses pytest, not unittest for Python tests

### Details
User corrected the AI when it tried to use unittest patterns. Project convention is pytest.

### Suggested Action
Use pytest fixtures and assertions. Run with `pytest` not `python -m unittest`.

output_examples.input: Agent realizes knowledge gap about API version
output_examples.output:
## [LRN-20260417-002] knowledge_gap

**Logged**: 2026-04-17T14:00:00Z
**Priority**: medium
**Status**: pending
**Area**: backend

### Summary
API v2 endpoint structure differs from v1

### Details
Assumed REST endpoints followed v1 patterns. User clarified v2 uses different conventions.

### Suggested Action
Check API version in docs before assuming endpoint structure. Consult user for version-specific patterns.

best_practices: Log entries promptly when events occur rather than waiting until end of session
Include specific context and suggested fixes to make entries actionable
Review learnings files before starting major tasks to avoid repeating past mistakes
anti_patterns: Logging full command output or error transcripts that may contain secrets
Overwriting existing entries instead of linking or updating priority
Capturing one-off issues without checking for similar existing entries first

faq.question: Where are the learnings stored?
faq.answer: Learnings are stored in markdown files in a .learnings/ directory at the project root. The skill creates LEARNINGS.md, ERRORS.md, and FEATURE_REQUESTS.md.

faq.question: Does this skill automatically fix errors?
faq.answer: No. This skill captures and documents errors, corrections, and insights. It does not execute fixes. The captured information helps future sessions avoid the same issues.

faq.question: How do I review past learnings?
faq.answer: Read the .learnings/ directory files directly, or ask the AI to summarize relevant entries from these files before starting a new task.

faq.question: What should I avoid logging?
faq.answer: Do not log secrets, tokens, private keys, environment variables with credentials, or full command output that may contain sensitive data. Use short summaries instead.

faq.question: Can I share learnings across projects?
faq.answer: Learnings are project-specific by default. For broadly applicable insights, promote entries to CLAUDE.md or other project documentation files.

faq.question: How do I create a new skill from a learning?
faq.answer: Use the helper script: ./scripts/extract-skill.sh <skill-name>. This creates a new skill file from a learning entry template.
